Not only Astrid Chevallier multi-talented composer, singer and musician, she also graphic artist. She designs movie posters. Her artistic talent evident her album, both sound and the cover art and materials that came with the record.
She French but sings mostly English. The music reminiscent Harvey's but also the more ethereal moody style someone like Kate Bush.
After Cirque downright creepy. The string intro with its heavy drum and Björkish vocal track gives chills. Fantastic. This track along with Pluie she does not sing lyrics but rather just vocalizes. She says "there's language have heard yet that could really express what going through songs like Pluie and After Cirque". Indeed. These are beautiful and scary, good way course.
This odd but complex and wonderful release.

The class 2012 and all its friends can breath collective sigh relief about least one thing May 11th approaches, thanks some timely research conducted the University Southern California.
According chief researchers, 98% the student body was under the impression that Commencement, "graduation" dirty slang terms, was death sentence, while the remaining were actually ousted robots programmed not recognize fear.
Fortunately for everyone, researchers were able successfully debunk this widely accepted belief taking five minutes call anyone that has ever graduated college before. What they learned was jarring, say the least.
turns out that fact, even after diplomas have been received, graduates living, because apparently graduating college isn't lethal act after all.
This all excellent news because for awhile, the Commencement diagnosis had hit our own USC family, namely myself and fellow Head Writer Leah Folta, and the future was uncertain until the recent breakthrough.
spite the reality that the class 2012 moving something different, you can rest assured that we're alive and well and living up.
Now that know we're not dying, that is.

Don Keough thinks Ireland should have Diaspora Minister, saying would transform Diaspora/Irish relations.
also wants see Notre Dame back Dublin playing football the next couple years. Tourism leaders will surely salivate after 30,000 fans showed the last time they played.
has just helped raise million for world class 1916 Rising documentary prepared Notre Dame and already bought RTE Public Television the and the BBC.
also believes that Ireland faced the worst crisis any western government had after the economic collapse and that American business leaders are keenly aware how well they have handled it.
the man who brought Notre Dame and Coca-Cola Ireland. also brought Bill Gates and Warren Buffett his lifelong friend too, hosting them the Club their first ever visit. serves Berkshire Hathaway's board with Buffett and still advisor the board Coca-Cola, the company served for years.
president Coca-Cola blazed the multinational trail Ireland the 1970s. Chairman the Board Notre Dame created the Keough-Naughton Irish Institute 1994 and their Dublin campus and laid the groundwork for the university play Dublin September 2012.
far from back number. Chairman the Board Allen and Company, the New York investment bank, and co-hosts their annual Sun Valley retreat attended world business leaders such Mark Zuckerberg, Bill Gates, Rupert Murdoch.
divides his time still between New York and Atlanta, his home, and keeps schedule younger man would surely tire of. Every year visits his beloved Ireland with his family and recently paid visit New Ross Wexford and the famine-era replica ship like the one his Michael embarked back the 1840s.
has come long way from Michael's lonely journey. Warren Buffet once stated that two men met business, Jack Welch and Don Keough, could have become president the United States they had chosen political rather than business career.
Keough demurs, but there little doubt that has been Ireland's greatest advocate America for decades now. His sense the potential the Diaspora was uppermost his mind during our New York interview.
How would you approach the Diaspora you were the Irish government?
person wants part that Diaspora the Irish government needs acknowledge that. good start would Minister for the Diaspora, who would charged with creating that welcome and outreach.
know when you become Irish citizen Ireland there event around it. you announce you are the Diaspora and coming Ireland that important too.
Israel has minister the Diaspora, read the New York Times, where and quote, "Over the next five years the Israeli government will spend $1.4 billion range initiatives strengthen Jewish identity abroad and Jewish connections Israel and vice versa." That the kind thinking need.
There should someone Minister who constant part the Diaspora, who reporting back cabinet.
asset that needs used. Just announce that minister would huge all over the world.
was delighted see Irish President Higgins mention and dedicate his trip the Irish Diaspora Britain his remarks during the state visit.
are everywhere across this world, this Irish army waiting help this small country their people left from.
Think million people worldwide, millions whom are seeking relationship with their ancestral home. What opportunity!
the Notre Dame game can bring 35,000 people Ireland what could massive outreach do? The Notre Dame game demonstrated what was possible. was the ultimate power the Diaspora that they didn't just come the game they captured the country.
was church last week Atlanta. couple who came the game came and told they were returning again, they fell utterly love with Ireland. They are just one small example what I'm saying.
What the upside potential for the Diaspora you think?
think within matter five years you would double the number people who would visit Ireland. That would goal.
needs preparation; Diaspora packages need put together. self-proclaimed member the Diaspora should feel like Notre Dame fans do, part something bigger, greater than the sum its parts. There should special discounts, meetings, and outreach.
Look what did with Notre Dame 35,000 came and there was massive range activities.
The visit Ireland always enjoyable, despite the weather sometimes. Ireland and what continues represent the best argument for itself.
What brought you there first your Irish roots?
Well, wanted bring children Ireland. father had gone late his life and was deeply moved it. I'll never forget how proud was go.
was the memory father. was the happiest trip his life, and said was upset didn't when was younger that was always mind. the first time went was when could afford it. father touched Ireland for me.
brought children and drove everywhere. This was the early 1970s. was excited. wanted get involved. remember years later reading your magazine Irish America and saying, finally, someone gets it.
You first created the links between the University Notre Dame and Ireland. There was very little connection until you came along. What made you want it?
Well, Notre Dame had extraordinary Irish background. Almost all the presidents had Irish connections. was just waiting connected into Ireland.
What made the Keough-Naughton Irish Institute was Professor Chris Fox, who had met, and told had amazing Irish collection books and major historical links and had deep interest Ireland. said, "Why don't you something?" Then the issue was how get started.
talked Seamus and said, "You have some amazing ideas about what Irish studies program should like we'll give you the blank canvas and the paints and you paint the Irish studies program you want the home the Irish America." golly, that was what exactly did.
One the most exciting things life has been see develop the way has. was perfect timing and Chris was great leader, and the university supported totally.
Some 10,000 students have studied there the past years and thousands Ireland. Then Martin Naughton came board partner. has been amazing.
Martin and his family played massive role the success Notre Dame game. became happening because Martin and was great that the Taoiseach was fully involved, and even got the weather!
always remember telling Martin about the idea the game and really getting the Irish involved. asked, "What took you long?" was huge boost the institute and have enlarged our efforts and numbers Ireland since.
You have great new Irish project well.
Yes, the 1916 landmark documentary series. BBC, RTE and PBS will show three-part landmark documentary about the 1916 Rising and have raised the money, almost million for it. are very excited about that. The series will run coincide with the centenary the Easter Rising.
What has Ireland meant you overall?
Ireland has been one the greatest experiences life. have such involvement there privilege. family loves and have all spent amazing times there. wanted buy house there, but wife Mickie said I'd never leave it.
How Ireland doing now your opinion?
the Kenny government had easy way solve the problems, and they had the toughest job any country the world.
They have done well. You hear lot respect for Ireland now over here. They faced the toughest problems and were the first demonstrate that they were working their way out it.
look what happened with lot pride how they have done it. has been very tough the Irish people know, but you have give them credit. I'm very optimistic about what going happen. lot smart Americans are getting involved there now, guys like John Malone.
Will you bring Notre Dame football back Ireland?
not but own view they will back. The call Ireland for Notre Dame very strong. Maybe within the next couple years.
Are you still bullish US?
When talk about the say never bet against it, these are the most resilient people the world.
have amazing gene pool collection people from all over the world who came not exploit but find new way grow and develop, many them were running away from poverty and oppression.
They could see hope here those that came. took enormous courage for people get and leave the first place.
I'm absolute optimist; own journey proves from small farm Iowa the presidency Coca-Cola.
Who you consider inspirational figures?
Father Ted Hesburgh Notre Dame. absolute inspiration; still simple priest. His most important moment his life when says Mass.
took him Latin America once. called several heads state. The minute brought him there they paid attention me. spoke them Portuguese, Spanish and charmed them all.
was incredible visionary who served six presidents issues such civil rights and immigration and built one the great universities. There were those who said Catholic university could never reach the heights academic excellence, but Father Hesburgh knew differently.
Warren Buffet still tap dances work every day. has arranged give all his wealth away, but he'd rich every important way with without it.
lives very simple life. have been friends for decades. lived across the street from him Omaha many years ago and still lives there, modest house, like the man.
Outside own father, the people have worked with Warren Buffett stands out. learning machine, great philanthropist. has kept amazing sense humility.
What the best life lesson you have learned?
The life lesson have learned that wealth has nothing with money. has with family, with friendships, with knowing and learning from interesting people, people who make difference. didn't have ten dollars I'd consider myself one the wealthiest people the world with the friends and family have had.
say grandkids, you need interesting person, and not just learn how move your thumbs around iPhone iPad. one wants around uninteresting people tell them. Young people today don't want vulnerable. They want cool, the worst possible thing be.
tell them you need through life saying this who comfortable skin, for better worse. Take for what am.
